ogTitle: Dutton Way vs Duverney: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Dutton Way and Duverney.
heading: Dutton Way vs Duverney — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Dutton Way, Victoria and Duverney, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Dutton Way has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Duverney (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Dutton Way or Duverney safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Dutton Way has a flood risk score of 53/100, while Duverney scores 52/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Dutton Way scores 32/100 for bushfire risk. Duverney scores 36/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Dutton Way or Duverney?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Dutton Way has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.85% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
